title: Contradiction Detector CLI
summary: Created contradiction_detector.py CLI tool that detects contradictions in N5 learnings using semantic similarity (sentence-transformers), pattern-based contradiction signals, and LLM verification for ambiguous cases. Implemented single-sided negation detection which catches contradictions like 'never ask about deadlines' vs 'ask about deadlines explicitly' that paired patterns miss. Integration function check_before_adding() available for use by other scripts.
success_criteria: Semantic similarity works (using sentence-transformers, already installed)
tests_passed: Check command detects contradiction: 'never ask about deadlines' vs 'ask about deadlines explicitly' (similarity 0.87, HIGH confidence)
Compare command detects contradiction: 'requires authentication' vs 'allows unauthenticated access'
Scan command works for system learnings and build learnings
check_before_adding() returns can_add=True for non-contradictory text

key_implementation_details.negation_detection: Single-sided (one text has negation, other doesn't) + paired patterns (require/allow, never/always, etc.)
key_implementation_details.llm_usage: Only for high-similarity pairs (>0.85) with no pattern signals

integration_notes.check_before_adding: Function available for import by other scripts. Returns dict with can_add, contradictions, action_required, reason.
integration_notes.pulse_learnings_integration: Currently prints '[Contradiction check] Detector not available, skipping check' - needs update to import and use check_before_adding(). This may be in D3.1 scope.

lessons_learned: Single-sided negation detection catches contradictions that paired patterns miss - one text has 'never/not/no' while similar text doesn't
Similarity threshold of 0.7+ avoids false positives while catching true contradictions
LLM verification adds value for high-similarity pairs without obvious pattern signals
